College Park · Atlanta, Georgia

We Must Make Our Community A Decent And Safe Place To Live.

— The Most Honorable Elijah Muhammad
“They will rebuild the ancient ruins and restore the places long devastated; they will renew the ruined cities that have been devastated for generations.” Isaiah 61:4 (NIV)

Project Torchlight is a nonprofit organizing fearless servants to rebuild the neighborhoods that built us. We champion families, inform the next generation, and serve our community through ten ministries of practical action.

Our work

Programs & Training

  • Self defense
  • How to Eat to Live and health
  • Trade and entrepreneurial training
  • God-centered marriages and family
  • Boys to Men teaching and training
  • Girls to Women teaching and training
  • And more

  • Mentorship
  • And more
From our brochure

What is Project Torchlight?

What is a Project?
A project is a planned, organized effort — a deliberate undertaking with a clear purpose, set in motion to achieve a specific goal.
What is Torchlight?
Torchlight is the illuminating flame of a torch — a guiding light that pierces the darkness and reveals the path forward for all who follow.
Together, Project Torchlight is an organized effort to bring light, knowledge, and guidance to the community we serve.

College View Hills Self-Improvement Program

Free community programs designed to develop the whole person — Boys to Men, Girls to Women, Self-Defense, Health & Wellness, Tutoring, Conflict Resolution, Family Strengthening, and our Self-Improvement Study Course. Programs begin March 18, 2026 at 7:00 PM.

Learn more
Latest news & upcoming events
See all
Summer 2026 · Every Wednesday 7–9 PM

College View Hills Summer Youth Leadership Program Begins

Empowering youth. Building leaders. Strengthening our community. Held at the Brady Recreation Center. See the official flyer below for full program details.
Beginning July 4, 2026 — God’s will

Saturday Community Outreach

Hot meals, fellowship, and conversation in the neighborhood. Open to everyone — bring a friend, a neighbor, a family member who needs assistance.
July 18, 2026 · College View Hills Apartments

Community Day & Family Cookout

Health and wellness screening and counseling from medical professionals, hot meals, grocery give-away, positive music, clothes give-away, children activities, and more. Youth Talent Showcase coming August 2026 — the winner wins $500!
Official summer program flyer

Summer Youth Leadership Program 2026

Empowering Youth. Building Leaders. Strengthening Our Community.

Every Wednesday from 7:00–9:00 PM at the Brady Recreation Center. Program focus areas: Boys to Men, Girls to Women, Trade & Apprenticeship Training, Health & Wellness, Self-Defense, Educational Tutoring, Conflict Resolution, Strengthening Family Values, and Self-Improvement & Leadership.

Sponsored by Councilwoman Jamelle McKenzie.

Free Community Programs

Eight programs running weekly through the College View Hills initiative — designed to develop the whole person and strengthen the family unit. Begins March 18, 2026 at 7:00 PM.

Youth Development

Boys to Men & Girls to Women

Teaching and training classes that prepare our young people for adulthood with discipline and identity.

Trades

Trade & Apprenticeship Training

Hands-on pathways into skilled work — building economic independence one craft at a time.

Defense

Self-Defense

Practical training so every member of the community can protect themselves and the people they love.

Health

How to Eat to Live

Nutrition, fitness, and wellness classes rooted in healthy bodies building healthy neighborhoods.

Education

Youth Educational Tutoring

Academic support for our students — closing gaps, building confidence, keeping the standard high.

Justice

Conflict Resolution & Mediation

Tools that turn disagreements into understanding — at home, at school, on the block.

Family

Strengthening the Family Unit

Programs that restore the bond at the center of every healthy community.

Discipline

Self-Improvement Study Course

A structured course for those ready to take responsibility for their own growth and development.

Our framework

The Ten Ministries

Every issue our community faces touches one of ten areas. We organize our work around all of them — because real change requires building everywhere at once.
